q: In solution layering, which layer determines the effective behavior of a component?
o: The unmanaged base always overrides managed
why: Managed layers stack over the unmanaged base; the top active layer determines behavior.

q: How does a managed UPGRADE differ from a managed UPDATE?
o: An upgrade replaces the solution and removes components deleted in the new version; an update patches in place
An upgrade only changes the theme
why: Upgrades fully replace (removing deleted components); updates/patches apply changes without removing.

q: Decide whether each layering/ALM statement is true.

rows.s: pac solution unpack turns a solution into source-control-friendly files.
Version control helps manage solution changes over time.
The unmanaged base sits above all managed layers.

why: Unpack aids source control; version control tracks changes; the unmanaged base is at the bottom, not top.

q: Which practice best supports healthy long-term solution architecture?
o: Version-controlled, layered managed solutions with automated ALM
One giant unmanaged solution edited in production
Screenshots as the source of truth
why: Layered managed solutions under version control with automated ALM is the healthy architecture.
